semanticCore: Fish, whale, the Arabic letter Nun, inkstand, or curved shape of a boat
quranicRelevance: Companion of the fish (Za al-noon - 'wa-za al-nooni').

keyDerivations.meaning: Fish, whale, massive sea creature, or the Arabic letter Nun

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَذَا النُّونِ إِذْ ذَهَبَ مُغَاضِبًا فَظَنَّ أَنْ لَنْ نَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ (21:87): And the Companion of the Fish (Jonah), when he went away in anger and thought We would not restrict him.
discardedNoise: Noon (middle of day) - English language homophone.
summaryEn: The root denotes a large fish or whale (Nun), designating Prophet Jonah as the 'Companion of the Fish' (Dhu al-Nun) due to his ocean ordeal.
summaryAr: تشير مادة هذا الجذر إلى الحوت والسمك العظيم في البحر، وحرف النون (نون)، ملقباً يونس بـ (ذا النون) لابتلاع الحوت له ونجاته بفضل تسبيحه.
rawText: نَوْنَةٌ The dimple in the chin of a young child: (M, K:) see خُنْعُبَةٌ, and قَلْتَةٌ, and دَائِرَةٌ, and 2 in art. دسم.
